title: APWLD Uprising / Art Rising Feminist Artivism Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded $3,000 Opportunity for Feminist Artists in Asia and the Pacific
summary: The Asia Pacific Forum on Women, Law and Development (APWLD) has officially opened applications for the 2026 Uprising / Art Rising: Feminist Artivism Fellowship, a unique regional opportunity designed for artists committed to feminist movements, social justice, and transformative storytelling through art. This groundbreaking fellowship invites artists from across Asia and the Pacific to collaborate with feminist activists, grassroots organisations, and regional movements to create powerful artistic works that amplify women’s lived realities and challenge structural inequalities. Selected fellows will receive a stipend of USD 3,000, mentorship, political grounding, regional exposure, and the opportunity to showcase their creative work in Bangkok, Thailand as part of the Uprising / Art Rising: A Feminist Art Space. The fellowship is ideal for artists working in visual arts, theatre, film, digital media, interdisciplinary arts, and other creative formats who are passionate about feminist advocacy, climate justice, labour rights, economic justice, food sovereignty, migration, and peace-building. About APWLD The Asia Pacific Forum on Women, Law and Development is one of the leading feminist networks in Asia and the Pacific. APWLD is a regional feminist, membership-driven organisation with more than 313 members from 31 countries and territories across Asia and the Pacific region. The organisation is committed to: Advancing women’s human rights Building feminist movements Promoting Development Justice Challenging structural inequalities Supporting grassroots activism Strengthening feminist solidarity across borders Through research, advocacy, movement-building, and creative engagement, APWLD works to address systemic issues affecting women and marginalised communities. About the Uprising / Art Rising Fellowship The Uprising / Art Rising: Feminist Artivism Fellowship was developed as an expansion of APWLD’s successful collaboration with artists and activists during the 2025 multidisciplinary exhibition titled “On Debt Row: A Multidisciplinary Art Exhibition on Debt and Its Impact on Lives in the Global South.” That exhibition, held in Sevilla, Spain, explored how debt and economic inequality affect women’s lives and communities across the Global South. Building on this success, APWLD is now launching a broader regional fellowship program that combines: Art activism Feminist movement-building Political education Grassroots collaboration Public engagement Creative advocacy The fellowship aims to support artists in co-creating meaningful art that reflects women’s voices, struggles, resistance, and lived experiences. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will gain access to extensive support and opportunities throughout the fellowship period. Financial Support Each selected fellow or team will receive: USD 3,000 stipend for project production and related expenses The funding will support creative work based on proposals co-developed and approved with APWLD. Additional Fellowship Benefits Participants will also receive: Political grounding and feminist analysis training Mentorship from APWLD programmes and partners Access to grassroots feminist organisations Regional networking opportunities Participation in feminist advocacy spaces Opportunities to attend APWLD activities and events Regional and international exposure for their work Exhibition opportunities in Bangkok, Thailand Promotion of their work through APWLD’s media and advocacy platforms Fellowship Duration The fellowship will run from: June 2026 to November 2026 Throughout this period, fellows will actively collaborate with APWLD and feminist movements across the region. Themes and Focus Areas APWLD is seeking artistic projects that creatively interpret pressing feminist and social justice issues. Applicants may focus on one or more of the following themes: 1. Feminist Economic Justice Artists may explore: Public and private debt Austerity measures Privatisation of public services Women’s livelihoods Resistance to illegitimate debt 2. Climate Justice Possible focus areas include: Feminist climate leadership Grassroots environmental movements Energy transitions Environmental defence Extractivist economies Corporate resistance to climate action 3. Peace and Justice Artists may address: Militarism Surveillance Occupation Repression of women human rights defenders Threats to feminist movements 4. Food Sovereignty Potential topics include: Agroecology Indigenous food systems Land rights Community food autonomy Resistance against industrial agriculture Corporate capture of food systems 5. Feminist Trade Justice Applicants may creatively explore: Trade systems Corporate power Women’s labour Economic inequality Human rights and trade intersections 6. Labour and Migration Projects may focus on: Women migrant workers Labour exploitation Migration systems Workers’ rights Gendered labour experiences Who Can Apply? The fellowship is open to artists from Asia and the Pacific region working in: Visual arts Theatre Film and video Digital arts Interdisciplinary arts Feminist creative activism Both individual and team applications are accepted. However, teams will still receive the same total stipend amount of USD 3,000. What Fellows Will Do Selected fellows will undertake a wide range of creative and collaborative activities during the fellowship. Key Responsibilities Include: Creating at least one major creative work or artwork Participating in virtual orientation and political grounding sessions Collaborating with APWLD programmes and grassroots partners Attending relevant APWLD events and advocacy spaces Travelling to Bangkok, Thailand in October 2026 Participating in the Uprising / Art Rising: A Feminist Art Space Submitting a documentation and financial report Producing social media content including: Photos Creative process updates One-minute video reels Participating in a final reflection meeting in November 2026 What is the Uprising / Art Rising: A Feminist Art Space? One of the most exciting aspects of the fellowship is participation in the Uprising / Art Rising: A Feminist Art Space in Bangkok. This physical gathering serves as: A collaborative feminist art space A site for experimentation and healing A platform for feminist storytelling A regional exhibition venue A community-building initiative The event showcases artistic works that amplify women’s voices and strengthen feminist struggles across Asia and the Pacific. Mentorship and Political Education A defining feature of this fellowship is its emphasis on political grounding and feminist analysis. Fellows will receive training and engagement opportunities around: Patriarchy Globalisation Fundamentalisms Militarism Feminist Development Justice Feminist movement-building Structural inequalities Each fellow will also be paired with APWLD programmes and partners relevant to their chosen theme. Selection Criteria Applicants will be evaluated based on: 1. Artistic Background Applicants must demonstrate experience in: Feminist art Social justice storytelling Human rights advocacy Grassroots movement engagement 2. Quality of Proposed Project Selection will consider: Creativity Feasibility Relevance to feminist themes Advocacy potential Community impact 3. Commitment to Feminist Movements Applicants should demonstrate alignment with feminist movement-building and social justice principles. 4. Language Skills Applicants must be: Proficient in English Able to communicate in at least one Asia-Pacific regional language 5. Travel Capacity Applicants must be able to travel internationally if required. Required Application Documents Interested applicants must submit: Completed application form Curriculum Vitae (CV) Project pitch/proposal Budget plan Sample work/portfolio Why This Fellowship Matters The APWLD Feminist Artivism Fellowship is more than a funding opportunity. It is a transformative platform that combines: Feminist politics Creative resistance Movement-building Regional solidarity Public advocacy Artistic freedom The fellowship empowers artists to challenge oppressive systems, amplify marginalised voices, and contribute meaningfully to feminist struggles across Asia and the Pacific. For artists committed to social justice and creative activism, this fellowship offers a rare opportunity to merge art, advocacy, and regional collaboration in a deeply impactful way. Eligible Applicants This opportunity is suitable for: Feminist artists Visual artists Theatre practitioners Filmmakers Video artists Digital artists Interdisciplinary artists Art activists Human rights creatives Grassroots cultural workers Qualifications Required Applicants should: Have experience in creative arts practice Demonstrate commitment to feminist movement-building Have experience working on women’s rights or development issues Be proficient in English Speak at least one Asia-Pacific regional language Be able to travel internationally if required Application Deadline 28 May 2026 How To Apply Click here for more and to apply Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid missing the deadline. For more global fellowship opportunities Click here The post APWLD Uprising / Art Rising Feminist Artivism Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded $3,000 Opportunity for Feminist Artists in Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mastercard Foundation DrPH Scholarships 2026–2027 at the University of Toronto for African Public Health Leaders
summary: Applications are now open for the Mastercard Foundation Doctor of Public Health (DrPH) Scholarships 2026–2027 at the University of Toronto in partnership with the Mastercard Foundation . This fully funded scholarship program is designed to support outstanding African scholars to pursue a Doctor of Public Health (DrPH) degree and develop into transformative leaders in public health, health systems strengthening, and data-driven health research. The initiative is aimed at equipping scholars with the expertise and leadership skills needed to return to Africa and drive impactful change in healthcare systems and public health policy across the continent. About the Scholarship Program The DrPH scholarship provides comprehensive financial support and academic development opportunities to selected candidates. The program emphasizes leadership, evidence-based practice, and practical solutions to public health challenges in Africa. Only up to three scholarships will be awarded for the 2026–2027 academic cycle, making this a highly competitive opportunity. Scholarship Benefits Selected scholars will receive full funding covering: Tuition fees International travel Accommodation Living expenses Books and academic materials Additional academic and transition support Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Be citizens and residents of Sub-Saharan African countries Have graduated from an Africa Health Collaborative partner institution Have not yet started a doctoral program Demonstrate strong academic excellence and leadership potential Meet admission requirements for the University of Toronto DrPH program Commit to returning to Africa after graduation to contribute to development Mandatory Information Session To remain eligible, applicants must attend a mandatory virtual information session . Failure to participate in this session may disqualify an applicant from consideration. Why This Opportunity Matters Public health leaders are critical to strengthening health systems and improving health outcomes across Africa. This scholarship invests in talented professionals who can apply advanced research and leadership skills to address the continent’s most pressing health challenges. Application Deadline No deadline has been specified in the announcement. Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible and monitor the official application portal for updates. How to Apply Apply here. For more Scholarship Opportunites , Visit OFY here. The post Mastercard Foundation DrPH Scholarships 2026–2027 at the University of Toronto for African Public Health Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Postdoctoral Research Fellow Opportunity: Research for Economic Security and Peace (RESP)
summary: Applications are now open for a Postdoctoral Research Fellow position with the Research for Economic Security and Peace (RESP) project. This is an excellent opportunity for early-career researchers interested in international political economy, economic security, peace, and cross-national data analysis. The successful candidate will work closely with Professor Yeling Tan , contributing to the empirical and analytical foundation of the RESP project. The role involves building and analyzing new cross-national datasets, producing high-quality research outputs, and supporting the coordination of a dynamic research team. About the RESP Project The Research for Economic Security and Peace (RESP) project examines how economic policies and international cooperation can promote peace and strengthen economic security across countries. The project combines rigorous data analysis with policy-relevant research to generate evidence that informs global debates on development, governance, and international relations. Key Responsibilities The Postdoctoral Research Fellow will: Construct and analyze new cross-national datasets Conduct advanced empirical and analytical research Contribute to academic publications and research outputs Provide administrative support to the project team Mentor and guide junior researchers Collaborate closely with Professor Yeling Tan and project partners Who Should Apply This role is ideal for researchers with strong quantitative and analytical skills and an interest in topics such as: Economic security Peace and conflict International political economy Development economics Data science and policy research Benefits of the Position Successful applicants will gain: Opportunity to work with a leading academic researcher Involvement in a high-impact international research project Experience in large-scale cross-national data construction Mentorship and collaboration within an interdisciplinary team Career development in academic and policy research Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by: 26 May 2026 at 12:00 noon (UK time) How to Apply Learn more about the project. Submit your application. For more Schorlarship opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post Postdoctoral Research Fellow Opportunity: Research for Economic Security and Peace (RESP) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: University of Johannesburg Post-Doctoral Fellowship 2026 in Welfare and Social Development for Researchers Worldwide
summary: The University of Johannesburg (UJ), through the South African Research Chair in Welfare and Social Development and the Centre for Social Development in Africa (CSDA), has officially opened applications for its prestigious 2026 Post-Doctoral Fellowship opportunity. This fully research-focused fellowship presents an excellent platform for emerging scholars and researchers who are passionate about advancing knowledge in social development, social protection, welfare systems, and public policy research. Hosted under the leadership of Professor Lauren Graham, the fellowship offers selected candidates the opportunity to spend 12 months contributing to impactful and policy-relevant research while strengthening their academic profile through publications, collaborations, and participation in ongoing projects. The fellowship may also be renewed based on satisfactory performance and continued funding availability. This opportunity is ideal for scholars who want to deepen their expertise in social policy and development research while working within one of Africa’s leading research environments focused on poverty, inequality, vulnerability, social welfare, and human development. The South African Research Chair Initiative (SARChI), which supports this fellowship, was established to strengthen research excellence and build future generations of scholars and researchers across South African universities. The Welfare and Social Development Chair at UJ has become an influential research hub dedicated to producing innovative and evidence-based solutions that address developmental challenges affecting vulnerable populations across Africa and beyond. The fellowship specifically seeks motivated and high-performing researchers whose work aligns with critical areas of contemporary social development research. Applicants with research interests in integrated social policy and social protection systems are highly encouraged to apply. Researchers working on issues related to precarious work and labour insecurity, digital inclusion in social protection systems, and the relationship between climate change and social policy are also strongly encouraged to submit applications. These research themes reflect some of the most urgent social and economic issues affecting communities globally. As governments and institutions continue to search for innovative responses to inequality, unemployment, technological exclusion, and climate vulnerability, the fellowship provides scholars with an opportunity to contribute to policy-relevant research capable of shaping real-world solutions. Successful fellows will become part of the Centre for Social Development in Africa (CSDA), a respected research centre at the University of Johannesburg known for its commitment to producing impactful social development research. The CSDA focuses on areas such as poverty reduction, inequality, social protection, youth development, disability inclusion, welfare policy, and developmental social welfare systems. One of the major expectations of the fellowship is scholarly publication. Fellows will be expected to publish at least three peer-reviewed journal articles derived from their doctoral research. In addition, they will actively contribute to ongoing research projects within the Chair and the CSDA. This makes the programme particularly beneficial for early-career academics who want to strengthen their research portfolio, improve their academic visibility, and build a strong publication record that can support future academic and professional opportunities. Beyond publications, fellows will gain access to a collaborative academic environment where they can engage with leading scholars, policy experts, and interdisciplinary researchers. This exposure can significantly improve their professional networks and future research collaborations. The University of Johannesburg itself is one of Africa’s leading universities, recognized internationally for academic excellence, innovation, and impactful research. Through initiatives such as SARChI, the university continues to contribute meaningfully to global conversations on development, welfare, social justice, and sustainable social policy. Applicants interested in this opportunity are advised to prepare their application materials early and ensure that their research interests clearly align with the fellowship themes. Strong academic writing skills, evidence of research competence, and a demonstrated commitment to social development research will strengthen applications considerably. Benefits of the Fellowship Selected fellows will benefit from: A 12-month post-doctoral research opportunity at the University of Johannesburg Possible renewal based on performance Mentorship and supervision from Professor Lauren Graham Opportunities to publish peer-reviewed journal articles Participation in impactful social development research projects Academic networking and collaboration opportunities Exposure to interdisciplinary and policy-focused research environments Strengthening of academic and professional research profiles Research Areas Applicants are encouraged to apply if their research relates to: Integrated social policy and social protection systems Social protection and precarious work Digital inclusion in social protection Social policy and climate change Application Deadline The deadline for applications is 30 May 2026. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here. title: Master in Cooperation and Development in Italy 2026/2027: Fully International Humanitarian Career Opportunity Now Open
summary: The internationally recognized Master in Cooperation and Development in Pavia, Italy, has officially opened applications for the 2026/2027 academic year. Organized by the University of Pavia and Almo Collegio Borromeo, this prestigious postgraduate program is designed for students and professionals who aspire to build impactful careers in humanitarian aid, international cooperation, development policy, NGOs, and global institutions. For individuals seeking to work with international organizations, research centers, development agencies, foundations, and humanitarian institutions, this program provides one of the most practical and globally connected pathways into the sector. The Master in Cooperation and Development has become one of Europe’s most respected international development programs because of its multicultural learning environment, intensive academic structure, practical field exposure, and extensive internship opportunities. Applications are currently open and interested candidates are encouraged to apply early before the deadline. Application Deadline Applications for the 2026/2027 edition are open until June 30, 2026. Early applications are strongly recommended. About the Program The Master in Cooperation and Development is a one-year postgraduate vocational program taught entirely in English. It combines academic learning with professional experience to prepare participants for careers in international development and humanitarian action. The program is hosted in Pavia, Italy, and attracts participants from different countries and cultural backgrounds each year, creating a highly diverse and international classroom experience. Students participate in: Advanced lectures and seminars Workshops and practical training sessions Career coaching and mentorship Multicultural teamwork Project management training Development economics and policy studies On-field learning experiences International internships The curriculum is specifically designed to help participants gain both theoretical knowledge and practical skills required in the humanitarian and development sectors. According to the University of Pavia, the program aims to train professionals capable of working in complex international contexts where economic, social, cultural, and political challenges intersect. Why This Program Stands Out One of the strongest aspects of the Master in Cooperation and Development is its practical orientation. Unlike many traditional academic programs, this master’s degree places strong emphasis on real-world exposure and professional readiness. Participants do not only study development theories but also learn how to design projects, manage humanitarian interventions, evaluate development programs, and collaborate across cultures. The program includes approximately 500 hours of teaching delivered by academics, international professionals, NGO practitioners, and experts from global organizations. Students also benefit from: International Networking Opportunities Participants interact with experts, humanitarian workers, development practitioners, and professionals from NGOs and international agencies. This helps students build strong professional networks that may open doors to future employment opportunities. Multicultural Learning Environment Each cohort includes students from different countries and cultural backgrounds. This diversity enriches discussions, teamwork, and learning experiences. Internship Placement One of the major highlights of the program is the final internship opportunity. Students can undertake internships lasting between three and six months with: NGOs International organizations Research institutions Foundations Public and private agencies Development institutions worldwide These internships provide hands-on field experience and significantly improve graduates’ employability in the international development sector. Career Coaching and Professional Guidance The program includes career orientation, internship coaching, and mentorship support to help students transition into international careers successfully. Scholarships and Financial Support Several scholarships are expected to be available for deserving candidates and applicants from low- and middle-income countries through the support of the Italian Agency for Development Cooperation (AICS). Financial support opportunities make the program more accessible to talented students and young professionals from developing regions. Tuition Fees The tuition fee for the 2026/2027 academic year is €8,000. This fee covers: Regular classes and seminars Teaching materials Tutorship support Internship guidance Career orientation Access to university facilities and libraries An application fee of €50 is also required during the application process. Program Duration The academic component of the program runs from November 2026 to June 2027, followed by internship placements and final thesis activities. Required Application Documents Applicants are generally required to submit: Application form Passport or identification document University degree certificate Academic transcripts CV or résumé Motivation letter Reference letter(s) Supporting documents for scholarship applications where applicable Applications are submitted online through the DreamApply platform. Career Opportunities After Graduation Graduates of the Master in Cooperation and Development often pursue careers in: International NGOs United Nations agencies Humanitarian organizations Development consulting firms Public policy institutions Government agencies Research organizations International advocacy institutions Deadline: June 30, 2026. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here. The post Master in Cooperation and Development in Italy 2026/2027: Fully International Humanitarian Career Opportunity Now Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Future Network Weimar Triangle 2026 Opens Applications for Research Stays in Germany for PhD Students and Postdocs
summary: The prestigious “Future Network Weimar Triangle: Science for Europe” programme is now accepting applications from PhD students and postdoctoral researchers from France and Poland who wish to undertake fully supported research stays in Germany. The initiative, coordinated by German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD), is designed to strengthen scientific collaboration and long-term European partnerships through interdisciplinary research, innovation, and academic exchange. The programme provides early-career researchers with the opportunity to spend between 3 and 24 months in Germany while participating in collaborative working groups focused on solving some of Europe’s most pressing technological, environmental, and societal challenges. The application deadline is 15 June 2026 . About the Future Network Weimar Triangle Programme The Weimar Triangle is a long-standing political and strategic cooperation framework established in 1991 by Germany, France, and Poland to promote European unity, collaboration, and shared development. Building on this vision, the “Future Network Weimar Triangle: Science for Europe” programme brings together talented researchers from the three countries to: Advance trilateral scientific cooperation Develop innovative research solutions for Europe’s future Encourage interdisciplinary collaboration Build sustainable international academic networks Promote knowledge exchange between emerging researchers The programme is funded by the German Federal Ministry of Research, Technology and Space and aligns with Germany’s High-Tech Agenda and broader European innovation priorities. Research Stay Duration Selected participants will conduct research stays in Germany lasting between: 3 months Up to 24 months The programme is designed to support meaningful long-term collaboration rather than short academic visits alone. Key Benefits of the Programme Participants selected for the Future Network Weimar Triangle programme will benefit from: 1. Research Opportunities in Germany Researchers will gain access to Germany’s advanced research ecosystem, universities, laboratories, and innovation networks. 2. International Networking Participants will collaborate with researchers and institutions across Germany, France, and Poland, helping them establish strong European professional networks. 3. Interdisciplinary Working Groups A central component of the programme is active participation in interdisciplinary working groups where researchers help shape research priorities and future scientific agendas. 4. Long-Term European Collaboration The initiative encourages sustainable partnerships that continue beyond the duration of the research stay. 5. Contribution to European Innovation Researchers will work on future-focused topics that address major European and global challenges. Research Areas and Priority Topics Applicants must conduct research related to one of the programme’s future-oriented thematic areas. These include: Climate-Neutral Energy and Mobility Research focused on: Sustainable energy systems Climate protection Green technologies Low-emission transportation Climate-neutral mobility solutions Artificial Intelligence, Digitalisation, and Microelectronics Topics may include: Artificial intelligence applications Emerging digital technologies Automation systems Data innovation Semiconductor and microelectronics research European Security and Research Resilience Research addressing: European resilience strategies Security systems Crisis preparedness Research infrastructure resilience Biotechnology and Future Medicine Areas of interest include: Medical innovation Biotechnology solutions Health technologies Future healthcare systems Societal Impact of New Technologies Researchers may also explore: Ethical implications of innovation Social transformation through technology Public policy and technology Benefits and challenges of emerging technologies Who Can Apply? The programme is open to: PhD students Exceptionally qualified postdoctoral researchers Applicants must be affiliated with eligible institutions and demonstrate strong interest in collaborative European research. The programme specifically targets researchers from: France Poland Germany Why This Programme Matters The Future Network Weimar Triangle programme is more than a traditional research mobility initiative. It is a strategic effort to strengthen Europe’s scientific future through collaboration between emerging researchers from three key European nations. Participants are not only conducting research; they are also helping shape future European scientific priorities through interdisciplinary cooperation and policy-relevant innovation. For ambitious early-career researchers seeking international exposure, high-level networking, and impactful research opportunities, this programme presents an exceptional platform. Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by: 15 June 2026 Applicants are encouraged to begin preparations early and carefully review eligibility requirements and programme details before applying. Learn more and apply: Interested candidates can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE for more information about the Asia Journalism Fellowship 2026. Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . The post Future Network Weimar Triangle 2026 Opens Applications for Research Stays in Germany for PhD Students and Postdocs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: IOC Launches 2027 PhD Students and Early Career Academics Research Grant Programme for Olympic Studies Researchers Worldwide
summary: The International Olympic Committee (IOC), through its renowned Olympic Studies Centre (OSC), has officially launched the 2027 edition of the PhD Students and Early Career Academics Research Grant Programme. The initiative is designed to support scholars conducting research related to the Olympic Movement, Olympism, the Olympic Games, athletes, sports history, and the wider social and cultural impact of the Olympics across the world. The programme continues the IOC’s long-standing commitment to advancing academic research and encouraging global scholarly engagement in Olympic studies. Since its establishment in 1999, the grant programme has supported more than 148 researchers from different countries, helping them pursue innovative academic projects while also providing opportunities to access the IOC’s unique collections and historical archives in Lausanne, Switzerland. For researchers interested in sports history, sociology, international relations, culture, politics, media, education, and other areas linked to Olympism and the Olympic Games, this programme presents a major opportunity to receive institutional recognition and financial support for their academic work. About the IOC Olympic Studies Centre Research Grant Programme The IOC Olympic Studies Centre serves as the world’s leading hub for Olympic knowledge and research resources. The centre was created to preserve, develop, and share information related to the Olympic Movement and its influence on society. Through the PhD Students and Early Career Academics Research Grant Programme, the IOC aims to: Encourage high-quality academic research on Olympism and the Olympic Movement Promote interdisciplinary studies related to sports and society Support young and emerging scholars globally Increase global understanding of Olympic values and their societal impact Facilitate access to the IOC’s historical archives and collections The grant programme is open to scholars whose research explores different dimensions of the Olympic Games and Olympic culture. Topics may include: Olympic history and heritage Athlete development and representation Sports governance and policy Olympic education and values Gender and diversity in sports Media and communication in the Olympic Movement Social, political, and economic impact of the Olympics Olympic legacy and sustainability International relations through sports diplomacy Cultural influence of the Olympic Games The programme particularly values research conducted from humanities and social sciences perspectives. Access to Unique Olympic Research Resources One of the major advantages of this programme is access to the IOC Olympic Studies Centre’s world-class research resources. Selected researchers may have the opportunity to visit Lausanne, Switzerland, where they can consult the IOC’s extensive collections, including primary sources and historical archives related to the Olympic Movement. Researchers will also gain access to the Olympic World Library (OWL), a specialized library catalogue dedicated entirely to Olympic knowledge. The library contains more than 42,000 official and academic publications, making it one of the largest Olympic-related academic collections in the world. The IOC believes that giving scholars access to these resources helps improve the quality and originality of academic research while preserving Olympic heritage for future generations. Who Can Apply for the 2027 Research Grant Programme? The programme is specifically targeted at postgraduate researchers and early career academics involved in Olympic-related studies. Eligible applicants include: 1. PhD Students Applicants must currently be enrolled in a PhD degree programme within the humanities or social sciences. Their research must include Olympism, the Olympic Movement, or the Olympic Games as at least one of the main areas of focus. 2. Early Career Academics Academic staff members and postdoctoral fellows are also eligible if they completed their doctorate or equivalent highest degree in or after 2024. In some academic fields, a master’s degree may also qualify as the highest equivalent degree. This broad eligibility framework allows emerging scholars from different disciplines and countries to participate. Application Process and Deadline Interested applicants are required to prepare and submit their application forms directly to the IOC Olympic Studies Centre . According to the official announcement, all applications and related correspondence must be submitted before: Deadline: Tuesday, 22 September 2026 Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the official programme rules, guidelines, and application form available on the IOC Olympic Studies Centre website before submitting their applications. The IOC has also announced that two online information sessions will be organized for researchers intending to apply. These sessions are expected to provide guidance on eligibility, application requirements, research expectations, and the overall selection process. Grant Selection Committee Applications will be reviewed by a Grant Programme Selection Committee composed of internationally recognized academic experts involved in Olympic studies, alongside representatives from the IOC Olympic Studies Centre. The committee evaluates applications based on: Academic quality Originality of the research topic Relevance to Olympic studies Methodological strength Potential contribution to the field This rigorous review process ensures that the programme supports impactful and high-quality academic work. Additional Olympic Research Opportunities Beyond the PhD and Early Career Academics Grant Programme, the IOC also offers the Advanced Olympic Research Grant Programme. This separate programme targets established researchers, including: University professors Lecturers Senior research fellows Applicants for the advanced programme must hold academic or research appointments and have completed their doctorate. The IOC has confirmed that applications for the 2027–2029 edition of the Advanced Olympic Research Grant Programme will open in October 2026. Why This Opportunity Matters The IOC research grant programme has become one of the most respected opportunities for scholars studying sports and society. It not only provides funding support but also offers researchers global academic visibility and access to exclusive Olympic resources. For young academics seeking to establish themselves in sports studies, sociology, history, or cultural research, this programme could significantly strengthen their academic profile and international research networks. Researchers interested in applying are encouraged to begin preparing their proposals early and participate in the upcoming online information sessions to better understand the application process. For more information, applicants can visit the IOC Olympic Studies Centre website or contact the centre directly via email at studies.centre@olympic.org . Opportunity Category Research Grant Eligible People and Regions Who Qualifies? Current PhD students Early career academics Academic staff members Postdoctoral fellows Researchers in humanities and social sciences Academic Qualifications Must be enrolled in a PhD programme OR Must have completed a doctorate or equivalent highest degree in or after 2024 Research must focus on Olympism, the Olympic Movement, or the Olympic Games Eligible Countries/Regions Open to applicants from all countries worldwide Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY here The post IOC Launches 2027 PhD Students and Early Career Academics Research Grant Programme for Olympic Studies Researchers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Accelerate Africa Startup Programme: Empowering African Founders to Build Global Businesses
summary: African startups continue to transform industries, create jobs, and solve some of the continent’s biggest challenges through innovation and technology. However, many early-stage founders struggle with mentorship, funding access, strategic guidance, and global market expansion. To bridge this gap, Accelerate Africa has emerged as one of the continent’s most promising startup accelerator platforms focused on helping visionary African entrepreneurs build scalable and globally competitive businesses. Accelerate Africa is a startup accelerator programme designed specifically for ambitious founders across Africa who are building innovative solutions capable of addressing major social, economic, and technological challenges. The programme provides founders with hands-on mentorship, strategic support, access to investors, and practical guidance from experienced operators who have built and scaled successful African startups. According to the official platform, Accelerate Africa’s mission is to accelerate Africa’s bold and visionary founders in the earliest stages of building global businesses that solve Africa’s biggest challenges. What is Accelerate Africa? Accelerate Africa is an intensive accelerator programme created to support early-stage African startups through mentorship, workshops, advisory services, and networking opportunities. Unlike many traditional startup accelerators, Accelerate Africa focuses heavily on personalized founder support rather than a one-size-fits-all programme structure. The accelerator selects a limited number of founders who receive direct coaching and guidance from experienced entrepreneurs, operators, investors, and advisors with deep experience across African startup ecosystems. The programme was founded with the vision of helping African entrepreneurs create world-class businesses capable of competing globally while solving pressing continental problems. Accelerate Africa has already supported startups and founders linked to some of Africa’s most successful companies, including: Andela Flutterwave Moove Daystar Power DukaConnect Many of these businesses have grown into globally recognized companies, raised millions of dollars in funding, or been acquired by international corporations. Key Benefits of the Accelerate Africa Programme The programme offers several valuable benefits to startup founders who are looking to scale their businesses and attract investors. 1. One-on-One Coaching and Mentorship Selected founders receive personalized coaching from experienced startup operators, investors, and advisors who understand the realities of building businesses in Africa. This mentorship helps founders improve critical business areas such as: Business strategy Leadership Product development Market positioning Customer acquisition Scaling operations Investor readiness 2. Access to Investor Networks Accelerate Africa connects startups with local and international investors through demo days and investor showcases. Founders gain exposure to venture capital firms, angel investors, ecosystem builders, and strategic partners who may provide funding opportunities after the programme. According to programme information, startups may become eligible for optional post-programme investment opportunities ranging from $250,000 to $500,000 through Future Africa and partner networks after due diligence and approval processes. 3. Workshops Across Core Startup Areas The accelerator programme includes practical workshops and office hours covering: Storytelling and brand positioning Team building and leadership Product-market fit Go-to-market strategies Revenue growth Fundraising preparation Legal support Financial management These sessions are designed to help founders avoid common startup mistakes while improving their chances of long-term success. 4. Networking and Founder Community Participants also gain access to a strong community of ambitious African founders facing similar entrepreneurial challenges. The programme encourages peer learning, collaboration, and long-term networking opportunities across multiple African countries. 5. No Immediate Equity Requirement One of the unique features of Accelerate Africa is that the programme does not immediately take equity from startups upon admission. This founder-friendly structure allows entrepreneurs to access support and mentorship without giving away ownership early in the process. Programme Structure and Timeline Accelerate Africa typically runs cohort-based accelerator programmes involving a small group of selected startups. The programme generally includes: Virtual mentorship sessions In-person welcome week activities Workshops and advisory clinics Demo day preparation Investor pitch events Some programme editions run for approximately 8 to 12 weeks depending on the cohort structure. The accelerator prioritizes quality over quantity by selecting only a small number of founders for each cohort. Why Accelerate Africa Matters for African Startups Africa’s startup ecosystem continues to grow rapidly, but many founders still struggle with access to funding, mentorship, and growth support during the early stages of business development. Accelerate Africa aims to close this gap by helping entrepreneurs: Build scalable businesses Improve operational systems Reach international markets Raise venture capital funding Develop sustainable business models Create jobs and economic impact The programme’s focus on practical founder support and African market realities makes it especially valuable for entrepreneurs operating in emerging and underserved markets. Accelerate Africa has also positioned itself as a platform committed to supporting diversity and inclusion across the startup ecosystem, including women founders and entrepreneurs from different African countries and backgrounds. Accelerate Africa Portfolio and Impact According to official programme statistics, Accelerate Africa and its broader ecosystem have supported: 162 companies 170 founders More than $2.92 billion in funds raised Over 1,900 applications received These numbers reflect the growing influence of the accelerator within Africa’s startup and venture capital ecosystem. How to Apply Interested founders can apply directly through the official Accelerate Africa platform. Applicants are encouraged to prepare: A strong startup pitch Clear problem-solution explanation Business traction evidence Team information Growth strategy Market opportunity analysis Due to the highly competitive nature of the programme, founders are advised to apply early and ensure their applications clearly demonstrate scalability, innovation, and impact potential. For more details and applications, founders can visit: Accelerate Africa Official Website Opportunity Category Accelerator Programme / Grant Support Opportunity Eligible People and Regions Who Can Apply? Early-stage startup founders African entrepreneurs Tech-enabled startups Innovative business founders Startup co-founding teams Qualifications Must be building a scalable startup Must solve a meaningful African challenge Must demonstrate growth potential Startups should show innovation and market opportunity Some cohorts may prioritize startups with traction or MVPs Eligible Countries/Regions Open to founders across Africa African startups from multiple countries are encouraged to apply Discover more global accelerate programs on OFY here The post Accelerate Africa Startup Programme: Empowering African Founders to Build Global Businesses appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: CIFAR Horizon Networks 2026: International Research Funding Opportunity for Early- and Mid-Career Researchers
summary: Applications are now open for the CIFAR Horizon Networks 2026, an exciting international research funding opportunity designed to support early- and mid-career researchers in building interdisciplinary and global research collaborations. Organised by Canadian Institute for Advanced Research , the Horizon Networks initiative aims to foster innovative research communities that can shape emerging fields of inquiry and establish the next generation of global thought leaders. The programme provides selected research teams with collaborative funding, professional development opportunities, international networking, and logistical support over a three-year period. Through Horizon Networks, researchers from diverse disciplines and institutions can work together to address some of the world’s most pressing challenges in education, childhood development, identity, technology, and civic engagement. About CIFAR Canadian Institute for Advanced Research , widely known as CIFAR, is a globally recognised research organisation headquartered in Canada. CIFAR brings together leading researchers from around the world to collaborate across disciplines and generate transformative ideas capable of solving complex global challenges. Supported by the governments of Canada, Alberta, and Québec, alongside international foundations, corporations, and partner organisations, CIFAR has built a strong reputation for advancing groundbreaking research and supporting academic innovation on a global scale. The organisation is particularly known for fostering interdisciplinary collaboration and creating environments where researchers can develop bold and innovative ideas that may not emerge within traditional academic structures. What Are Horizon Networks? Horizon Networks are collaborative international research networks led by early- to mid-career researchers. These networks are designed to: Build emerging interdisciplinary research communities Explore new fields of inquiry Strengthen international academic collaboration Develop future research leaders Encourage innovative thinking across disciplines Each selected Horizon Network will operate over a three-year term and receive support from CIFAR to pursue collaborative research activities. What the Programme Offers Selected Horizon Networks receive extensive support and funding, including: Three-Year Research Network Support Networks operate over a three-year period with long-term collaborative engagement among members. In-Person Meetings Network members meet twice per year in person, with all logistics, financial arrangements, and administrative coordination managed by CIFAR. Collaborative Research Funding Each Horizon Network receives funding distributed across the team to support collaborative research activities and network initiatives. Flexible Fund Management While CIFAR distributes the funding, Horizon Network leads are responsible for allocating and managing the funds among team members. Professional Development All network members participate in annual professional development opportunities designed to strengthen leadership, collaboration, and research impact. International and Interdisciplinary Collaboration Researchers gain access to international academic networks and interdisciplinary partnerships that can significantly enhance research visibility and long-term career growth. Eligibility Requirements The Horizon Networks programme specifically targets emerging research leaders and interdisciplinary collaboration. Leadership Requirements Each network must be co-led by researchers who are alumni of CIFAR’s early-career leadership programmes. Team Composition Each Horizon Network may include: Up to eight additional early- to mid-career faculty members Researchers from diverse institutions and disciplines Individuals who may not have previously participated in CIFAR programmes Institutional Diversity Network co-leaders are encouraged to recruit members from a broad range of institutions to ensure diversity of perspectives and expertise. Current Horizon Networks Streams for 2026 Applicants must submit proposals under one of the following thematic streams. Stream 1: Belonging with Nature, Technology and People in Early Childhood Supported by the Bezos Family Foundation, this stream explores how children’s relationships with nature, technology, and social environments shape development and belonging during early childhood. Stream 2: Adolescence – Belonging, Identity and Civic Engagement in a Changing World Also supported by the Bezos Family Foundation, this stream focuses on adolescent identity formation, civic participation, and social belonging in rapidly evolving societal contexts. Stream 3: Education and Learning Variability Between Ages 2–12 This stream is supported by the Jacobs CIFAR Research Fellowship programme and must be co-led by current or former Jacobs CIFAR Research Fellows. The stream investigates learning variability and educational development among children aged 2 to 12. Why This Opportunity Matters The Horizon Networks programme stands out because it empowers researchers at relatively early stages of their careers to: Lead international research collaborations Shape emerging academic fields Develop leadership and management skills Build influential research communities Access international visibility and funding opportunities Rather than supporting individual projects alone, the programme invests in building sustainable research ecosystems that can continue generating impact beyond the funding period. Review and Selection Process Applications undergo a multi-stage review process. Eligibility Screening CIFAR staff first review all submissions to ensure eligibility requirements are met. Reciprocal Peer Review Applicants also participate in a reciprocal peer review process, where proposed Horizon Network leads review and evaluate other applications. Final Decision Final funding decisions are made by CIFAR together with funding partners where applicable. Applicants submitting proposals agree to: Review assigned proposals Participate in peer evaluation Engage in the collaborative assessment process Applications are scored using a rubric aligned with the official selection criteria. Key Dates for CIFAR Horizon Networks 2026 Applications Open 6 May 2026 Application Deadline 17 June 2026 at 12 PM ET Peer Review Deadline 15 July 2026 Decision Notifications Summer 2026 Applicants are encouraged to begin building interdisciplinary teams and developing strong collaborative proposals early. Ideal Applicant Profile This opportunity is particularly suitable for: Early- and mid-career faculty researchers Interdisciplinary scholars Researchers interested in collaborative international work Academics exploring emerging research questions Scholars focused on childhood, education, technology, identity, civic engagement, and human development Strong proposals will typically demonstrate: Innovative interdisciplinary thinking Clear collaborative structures Leadership potential Strong research vision Global relevance and societal impact Benefits of Participating in Horizon Networks Researchers selected for Horizon Networks gain access to: International research collaborations Leadership development opportunities Funding flexibility Academic visibility Long-term interdisciplinary partnerships Exposure to high-level global research communities The programme also creates valuable opportunities for future grants, publications, policy influence, and large-scale research initiatives. Final Thoughts The CIFAR Horizon Networks 2026 programme offers a unique platform for emerging academic leaders to build influential interdisciplinary research collaborations on a global scale. Through funding, mentorship, networking, and professional development, the programme helps researchers shape new fields of inquiry while addressing important societal challenges. For early- and mid-career researchers seeking to establish themselves as international thought leaders, Horizon Networks represents a highly valuable and prestigious opportunity. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post CIFAR Horizon Networks 2026: International Research Funding Opportunity for Early- and Mid-Career Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded BIOFABRICATE PhD Studentship 2026 at Trinity College Dublin: Research Opportunity in Pharmaceutical Sciences and Sustainable Biomaterials
summary: Applications are now open for the fully funded BIOFABRICATE PhD Studentship 2026 at Trinity College Dublin , one of Europe’s most prestigious and internationally recognised universities. This exciting doctoral research opportunity is hosted within the School of Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences at Trinity College Dublin and is funded by the Department of Agriculture, Food and the Marine (DAFM) in Ireland. The PhD studentship forms part of the larger BIOFABRICATE programme, a groundbreaking multi-institutional research initiative focused on developing sustainable bio-based materials and technologies from Irish forest biomass for applications across pharmaceuticals, construction, manufacturing, and packaging industries. This opportunity is ideal for candidates interested in pharmaceutical sciences, biomaterials, chemistry, sustainable manufacturing, and advanced pharmaceutical formulation research. About Trinity College Dublin Founded in 1592, Trinity College Dublin is Ireland’s leading university and one of the oldest and most respected institutions in Europe. Ranked 75th globally in the QS World University Rankings 2026, Trinity has built a strong international reputation for excellence in education, research, innovation, and global impact. The university is known for: World-class research facilities Strong international academic collaborations Leadership in sustainability and innovation Inclusive and diverse academic environment Commitment to equality, diversity, and work-life balance Trinity College Dublin also holds the Athena Swan Silver Award and is recognised as an EU Sustainable Gender Equality Champion. About the BIOFABRICATE Research Programme BIOFABRICATE stands for: Integrated Bio-based Innovations for Sustainable Manufacturing, Construction, and Pharmaceutical Applications from Irish Forest Biomass The programme is led by the University of Galway and involves collaboration with several Irish institutions, including: Trinity College Dublin Technological University Dublin Atlantic Technological University Dublin City University Teagasc Multiple industry partners The programme focuses on transforming Ireland’s growing forest biomass resources into advanced sustainable materials that can be applied across various industrial sectors. PhD Research Project Overview The selected PhD candidate will work under the supervision of Professor Anne Marie Healy in the School of Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences, with co-supervision from Dr. Ramesh Babu in the School of Chemistry. The project title is: “Evaluation of the Use of Microcrystalline Cellulose Generated from Irish Forestry Biomass in Pharmaceutical Solid Dosage Forms” The research will investigate the properties and pharmaceutical performance of microcrystalline cellulose (MCC) produced from Irish forest biomass (IFB-MCC). The project aims to: Evaluate the physical and chemical properties of IFB-MCC Compare IFB-MCC with commercially available pharmaceutical excipients Assess suitability for tablet direct compression applications Explore sustainable alternatives for pharmaceutical manufacturing Support innovation in environmentally friendly pharmaceutical materials This interdisciplinary project combines pharmaceutical sciences, biomaterials research, chemistry, and sustainability. What the Studentship Offers The fully funded PhD studentship provides candidates with: Full doctoral research funding Opportunity to work within a major national research programme Access to advanced laboratories and research infrastructure Collaboration with leading researchers and institutions Exposure to interdisciplinary and industry-linked research Academic mentorship from internationally recognised supervisors Opportunity to contribute to sustainable pharmaceutical innovation The position also offers valuable research and professional development opportunities within a collaborative academic environment. Ideal Candidate Profile The programme is suitable for highly motivated applicants with backgrounds in: Pharmaceutical Sciences Pharmacy Chemistry Materials Science Biomedical Sciences Chemical Engineering Pharmaceutical Technology Related scientific disciplines Candidates with research experience in pharmaceutical formulations, biomaterials, analytical chemistry, or sustainable materials may have an advantage. Application Requirements Applicants are required to submit the following documents via email to Professor Anne Marie Healy: Required Documents Full Curriculum Vitae (CV) A short motivation statement (maximum one page) outlining: Interest in the project Understanding of the research topic Academic and research motivations A writing sample such as: Published paper Thesis excerpt Academic report Contact details of two referees, including email addresses Application Deadline The application deadline is: 25 May 2026 at 12 noon (Irish time) Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews in June 2026. Trinity College Dublin’s Commitment to Inclusion Trinity College Dublin strongly values equality, diversity, and inclusion and welcomes applications from: International students Applicants with disabilities Individuals from non-traditional academic backgrounds Researchers displaced due to war or conflict Applicants with career breaks or caring responsibilities The university is committed to fostering an inclusive and supportive research environment where all scholars can thrive. Why This PhD Opportunity Stands Out This fully funded PhD studentship is particularly attractive because it combines: High-impact sustainability research Pharmaceutical innovation Interdisciplinary collaboration Industry relevance International academic exposure Access to leading Irish research institutions The project also aligns with growing global priorities surrounding sustainable manufacturing and environmentally responsible pharmaceutical development. Career Opportunities After the PhD Graduates completing this research programme may pursue careers in: Pharmaceutical research and development Sustainable materials innovation Academia and scientific research Biotechnology industries Pharmaceutical manufacturing Regulatory and formulation sciences Biomaterials and green chemistry sectors The interdisciplinary nature of the project equips researchers with highly transferable technical and analytical skills. Final Thoughts The BIOFABRICATE PhD Studentship 2026 at Trinity College Dublin offers an outstanding opportunity for aspiring researchers passionate about pharmaceutical sciences, sustainability, and biomaterials innovation. With full funding, expert supervision, collaborative research networks, and the chance to contribute to sustainable pharmaceutical technologies, this doctoral programme represents an excellent pathway for candidates seeking impactful research careers. Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare strong application materials and apply before the deadline.
